original_hypothesis.title: De-Whitelisting Market Permanently Locks Accrued User Rewards with No Emergency Claim Path
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: When governance de-whitelists a market via `whiteListLendingMarket(_market, false)`, the `update_market` function reverts with 'Market not whitelisted'. Since both `claim()` and `sync_ledger()` call `update_market` first, ALL operations for the market are permanently blocked. Users with accrued but unclaimed CANTO rewards lose access with no emergency withdrawal or grace period mechanism.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a design-level deficiency where governance has the power to irreversibly lock user funds. While documented trust assumptions say governance is non-malicious, there is no grace period, emergency claim function, or user notification mechanism. Even accidental de-whitelisting with immediate re-whitelisting resets lastRewardBlock, potentially skipping reward periods.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Line 56: `require(lendingMarketWhitelist[_market])` blocks all operations. Line 117-122: de-whitelisting provides no grace period. No emergency claim function exists.

preconditions: Governance de-whitelists a market with active users having unclaimed rewards

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Whitelist check gates all operations with no bypass for existing claims
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: De-whitelisting instantly blocks claim() and sync_ledger(). If lending market depends on sync_ledger for user withdrawals, user deposits in the actual market may also be stuck.
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No emergency claim function, no grace period, no state preservation on de-whitelist

analysis_questions: Can governance re-whitelist to allow claims, and does this correctly preserve reward accounting?
Does the lending market implementation handle sync_ledger reverts gracefully?
What is the total value at risk across all active markets?
validation_requirements: Test de-whitelist → re-whitelist cycle and verify claim correctness
Verify lending market behavior when sync_ledger reverts
Assess whether re-whitelisting creates accounting gaps

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Governance de-whitelists a market (accidentally or due to policy change). Users with pending CANTO rewards are immediately locked out. The lending market may also be unable to process withdrawals if it requires sync_ledger to succeed. Even re-whitelisting creates issues (TECH-H007: stale state, lastRewardBlock reset).
prerequisites: Governance de-whitelists a market with active users

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: All accrued unclaimed CANTO rewards for the market are permanently inaccessible
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users with unclaimed rewards in the de-whitelisted market
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Partial recovery via re-whitelisting but with accounting inconsistencies

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for user claim behavior after market de-whitelisting

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Immediately visible when users attempt to claim

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
